verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est autem in illa die persecutio magna in ecclesia quæ erat Jerosolymis, et omnes dispersi sunt per regiones Judææ et Samariæ præter Apostolos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σαῦλος δὲ ἦν συνευδοκῶν τῇ ἀναιρέσει αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ διωγμὸς μέγας ἐπὶ τὴν ἐκκλησίαν τὴν ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις πάντες τε διεσπάρησαν κατὰ τὰς χώρας τῆς ἰουδαίας καὶ σαμαρείας πλὴν τῶν ἀποστόλων
verses.king_james: And Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judaea and Samaria, except the apostles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Curaverunt autem Stephanum viri timorati, et fecerunt planctum magnum super eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνεκόμισαν δὲ τὸν στέφανον ἄνδρες εὐλαβεῖς καὶ ἐποίησαντὸ κοπετὸν μέγαν ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And devout men carried Stephen *to his burial*, and made great lamentation over him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Saulus autem devastabat ecclesiam per domos intrans, et trahens viros ac mulieres, tradebat in custodiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σαῦλος δὲ ἐλυμαίνετο τὴν ἐκκλησίαν κατὰ τοὺς οἴκους εἰσπορευόμενος σύρων τε ἄνδρας καὶ γυναῖκας παρεδίδου εἰς φυλακήν
verses.king_james: As for Saul, he made havock of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed *them* to prison.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur qui dispersi erant pertransibant, evangelizantes verbum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ μὲν οὖν διασπαρέντες διῆλθον εὐαγγελιζόμενοι τὸν λόγον
verses.king_james: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.

verses.latin_vulgate: Philippus autem descendens in civitatem Samariæ, prædicabant illis Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φίλιππος δὲ κατελθὼν εἰς πόλιν τῆς σαμαρείας ἐκήρυσσεν αὐτοῖς τὸν χριστόν
verses.king_james: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intendebant autem turbæ his quæ a Philippo dicebantur, unanimiter audientes, et videntes signa quæ faciebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσεῖχον τὲ οἱ ὄχλοι τοῖς λεγομένοις ὑπὸ τοῦ φιλίππου ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐν τῷ ἀκούειν αὐτοὺς καὶ βλέπειν τὰ σημεῖα ἃ ἐποίει
verses.king_james: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi enim eorum qui habebant spiritus immundos, clamantes voce magna exibant. Multi autem paralytici et claudi curati sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλῶν γὰρ τῶν ἐχόντων πνεύματα ἀκάθαρτα βοῶντα μεγάλῃ φωνῇ ἐξήρχετο πολλοὶ δὲ παραλελυμένοι καὶ χωλοὶ ἐθεραπεύθησαν
verses.king_james: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed *with them*: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est ergo gaudium magnum in illa civitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο χαρὰ μεγάλη ἐν τῇ πόλει ἐκείνῃ
verses.king_james: And there was great joy in that city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir autem quidam nomine Simon, qui ante fuerat in civitate magnus, seducens gentem Samariæ, dicens se esse aliquem magnum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνὴρ δέ τις ὀνόματι σίμων προϋπῆρχεν ἐν τῇ πόλει μαγεύων καὶ ἐξιστῶν τὸ ἔθνος τῆς σαμαρείας λέγων εἶναί τινα ἑαυτὸν μέγαν
verses.king_james: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:

verses.latin_vulgate: cui auscultabant omnes a minimo usque ad maximum, dicentes: Hic est virtus Dei, quæ vocatur magna.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ προσεῖχον πάντες ἀπὸ μικροῦ ἕως μεγάλου λέγοντες οὗτός ἐστιν ἡ δύναμις τοῦ θεοῦ ἡ μεγάλη
verses.king_james: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendebant autem eum: propter quod multo tempore magiis suis dementasset eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσεῖχον δὲ αὐτῷ διὰ τὸ ἱκανῷ χρόνῳ ταῖς μαγείαις ἐξεστακέναι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum vero credidissent Philippo evangelizanti de regno Dei, in nomine Jesu Christi baptizabantur viri ac mulieres.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἐπίστευσαν τῷ φιλίππῳ εὐαγγελιζομένῳ τὰ περὶ τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τοῦ ὀνόματος τοῦ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ἐβαπτίζοντο ἄνδρες τε καὶ γυναῖκες
verses.king_james: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc Simon et ipse credidit: et cum baptizatus esset, adhærebat Philippo. Videns etiam signa et virtutes maximas fieri, stupens admirabatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ σίμων καὶ αὐτὸς ἐπίστευσεν καὶ βαπτισθεὶς ἦν προσκαρτερῶν τῷ φιλίππῳ θεωρῶν τε σημεῖα καὶ δυνάμεις μεγάλας γινομένας ἐξίστατο
verses.king_james: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem audissent Apostoli qui erant Jerosolymis, quod recepisset Samaria verbum Dei, miserunt ad eos Petrum et Joannem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσαντες δὲ οἱ ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις ἀπόστολοι ὅτι δέδεκται ἡ σαμάρεια τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ ἀπέστειλαν πρὸς αὐτοὺς τὸν πέτρον καὶ ἰωάννην
verses.king_james: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum venissent, oraverunt pro ipsis ut acciperent Spiritum Sanctum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες καταβάντες προσηύξαντο περὶ αὐτῶν ὅπως λάβωσιν πνεῦμα ἅγιον
verses.king_james: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:

verses.latin_vulgate: nondum enim in quemquam illorum venerat, sed baptizati tantum erant in nomine Domini Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔπω γὰρ ἦν ἐπ᾽ οὐδενὶ αὐτῶν ἐπιπεπτωκός μόνον δὲ βεβαπτισμένοι ὑπῆρχον εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc imponebant manus super illos, et accipiebant Spiritum Sanctum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐπετίθουν τὰς χεῖρας ἐπ᾽ αὐτούς καὶ ἐλάμβανον πνεῦμα ἅγιον
verses.king_james: Then laid they *their* hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum vidisset autem Simon quia per impositionem manus Apostolorum daretur Spiritus Sanctus, obtulit eis pecuniam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεασάμενος δὲ ὁ σίμων ὅτι διὰ τῆς ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν τῶν ἀποστόλων δίδοται τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον, προσήνεγκεν αὐτοῖς χρήματα
verses.king_james: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles' hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Date et mihi hanc potestatem, ut cuicumque imposuero manus, accipiat Spiritum Sanctum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων, δότε κἀμοὶ τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην ἵνα ᾧ ἂν ἐπιθῶ τὰς χεῖρας λαμβάνῃ πνεῦμα ἅγιον
verses.king_james: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus autem dixit ad eum: Pecunia tua tecum sit in perditionem: quoniam donum Dei existimasti pecunia possideri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέτρος δὲ εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν τὸ ἀργύριόν σου σὺν σοὶ εἴη εἰς ἀπώλειαν ὅτι τὴν δωρεὰν τοῦ θεοῦ ἐνόμισας διὰ χρημάτων κτᾶσθαι
verses.king_james: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est tibi pars neque sors in sermone isto: cor enim tuum non est rectum coram Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν σοι μερὶς οὐδὲ κλῆρος ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τούτῳ ἡ γὰρ καρδία σου οὐκ ἔστιν εὐθεῖα ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pœnitentiam itaque age ab hac nequitia tua: et roga Deum, si forte remittatur tibi hæc cogitatio cordis tui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετανόησον οὖν ἀπὸ τῆς κακίας σου ταύτης καὶ δεήθητι τοῦ θεοῦ, εἰ ἄρα ἀφεθήσεταί σοι ἡ ἐπίνοια τῆς καρδίας σου
verses.king_james: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: In felle enim amaritudinis, et obligatione iniquitatis, video te esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς γὰρ χολὴν πικρίας καὶ σύνδεσμον ἀδικίας ὁρῶ σε ὄντα
verses.king_james: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and *in* the bond of iniquity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Simon, dixit: Precamini vos pro me ad Dominum, ut nihil veniat super me horum quæ dixistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ σίμων εἶπεν δεήθητε ὑμεῖς ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ πρὸς τὸν κύριον ὅπως μηδὲν ἐπέλθῃ ἐπ᾽ ἐμὲ ὧν εἰρήκατε
verses.king_james: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et illi quidem testificati, et locuti verbum Domini, redibant Jerosolymam, et multis regionibus Samaritanorum evangelizabant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ μὲν οὖν διαμαρτυράμενοι καὶ λαλήσαντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ κυρίου ὑπέστρεψαν εἰς ἰερουσαλήμ πολλάς τε κώμας τῶν σαμαρειτῶν εὐηγγελίσαντο
verses.king_james: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.

verses.latin_vulgate: Angelus autem Domini locutus est ad Philippum, dicens: Surge, et vade contra meridianum, ad viam quæ descendit ab Jerusalem in Gazam: hæc est deserta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄγγελος δὲ κυρίου ἐλάλησεν πρὸς φίλιππον λέγων, ἀνάστηθι καὶ πορεύου κατὰ μεσημβρίαν ἐπὶ τὴν ὁδὸν τὴν καταβαίνουσαν ἀπὸ ἰερουσαλὴμ εἰς γάζαν αὕτη ἐστὶν ἔρημος
verses.king_james: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surgens abiit. Et ecce vir Æthiops, eunuchus, potens Candacis reginæ Æthiopum, qui erat super omnes gazas ejus, venerat adorare in Jerusalem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναστὰς ἐπορεύθη καὶ ἰδού, ἀνὴρ αἰθίοψ εὐνοῦχος δυνάστης κανδάκης τῆς βασιλίσσης αἰθιόπων ὃς ἦν ἐπὶ πάσης τῆς γάζης αὐτῆς ὃς ἐληλύθει προσκυνήσων εἰς ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,

verses.latin_vulgate: et revertebatur sedens super currum suum, legensque Isaiam prophetam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν τε ὑποστρέφων καὶ καθήμενος ἐπὶ τοῦ ἅρματος αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀνεγίνωσκεν τὸν προφήτην ἠσαΐαν
verses.king_james: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Spiritus Philippo: Accede, et adjunge te ad currum istum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ τὸ πνεῦμα τῷ φιλίππῳ πρόσελθε καὶ κολλήθητι τῷ ἅρματι τούτῳ
verses.king_james: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accurrens autem Philippus, audivit eum legentem Isaiam prophetam, et dixit: Putasne intelligis quæ legis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσδραμὼν δὲ ὁ φίλιππος ἤκουσεν αὐτοῦ ἀναγινώσκοντος τὸν προφήτην ἠσαΐαν καὶ εἶπεν Ἆρά γε γινώσκεις ἃ ἀναγινώσκεις
verses.king_james: And Philip ran thither to *him*, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait: Et quomodo possum, si non aliquis ostenderit mihi? Rogavitque Philippum ut ascenderet, et sederet secum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν πῶς γὰρ ἂν δυναίμην ἐὰν μή τις ὁδήγησῃ με παρεκάλεσέν τε τὸν φίλιππον ἀναβάντα καθίσαι σὺν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locus autem Scripturæ quem legebat, erat hic: [Tamquam ovis ad occisionem ductus est: et sicut agnus coram tondente se, sine voce, sic non aperuit os suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ περιοχὴ τῆς γραφῆς ἣν ἀνεγίνωσκεν ἦν αὕτη ὡς πρόβατον ἐπὶ σφαγὴν ἤχθη καὶ ὡς ἀμνὸς ἐναντίον τοῦ κείροντος αὐτὸν ἄφωνος οὕτως οὐκ ἀνοίγει τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:

verses.latin_vulgate: In humilitate judicium ejus sublatum est. Generationem ejus quis enarrabit? quoniam tolletur de terra vita ejus.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ ταπεινώσει αὐτοῦ ἡ κρίσις αὐτοῦ ἤρθη τὴν δὲ γενεὰν αὐτοῦ τίς διηγήσεται ὅτι αἴρεται ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς ἡ ζωὴ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem eunuchus Philippo, dixit: Obsecro te, de quo propheta dicit hoc? de se, an de alio aliquo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ εὐνοῦχος τῷ φιλίππῳ εἶπεν δέομαί σου περὶ τίνος ὁ προφήτης λέγει τοῦτο περὶ ἑαυτοῦ ἢ περὶ ἑτέρου τινός
verses.king_james: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperiens autem Philippus os suum, et incipiens a Scriptura ista, evangelizavit illi Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνοίξας δὲ ὁ φίλιππος τὸ στόμα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀρξάμενος ἀπὸ τῆς γραφῆς ταύτης εὐηγγελίσατο αὐτῷ τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dum irent per viam, venerunt ad quamdam aquam: et ait eunuchus: Ecce aqua: quid prohibet me baptizari?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἐπορεύοντο κατὰ τὴν ὁδόν ἦλθον ἐπί τι ὕδωρ καί φησιν ὁ εὐνοῦχος ἰδού, ὕδωρ τί κωλύει με βαπτισθῆναι
verses.king_james: And as they went on *their* way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, *here is* water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Philippus: Si credis ex toto corde, licet. Et respondens ait: Credo Filium Dei esse Jesum Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ φίλιππος εἰ πιστεύεις ἐξ ὅλης τὴς καρδίας, ἔξεστιν ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ εἶπεν πιστεύω τὸν ὑιὸν τοῦ θεοῦ ἐιναι τὸν ἰησοῦν χριστόν
verses.king_james: And Philip said, If thou believest with all thine heart, thou mayest. And he answered and said, I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et jussit stare currum: et descenderunt uterque in aquam, Philippus et eunuchus, et baptizavit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκέλευσεν στῆναι τὸ ἅρμα καὶ κατέβησαν ἀμφότεροι εἰς τὸ ὕδωρ ὅ τε φίλιππος καὶ ὁ εὐνοῦχος καὶ ἐβάπτισεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem ascendissent de aqua, Spiritus Domini rapuit Philippum, et amplius non vidit eum eunuchus. Ibat autem per viam suam gaudens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἀνέβησαν ἐκ τοῦ ὕδατος πνεῦμα κυρίου ἥρπασεν τὸν φίλιππον καὶ οὐκ εἶδεν αὐτὸν οὐκέτι ὁ εὐνοῦχος ἐπορεύετο γὰρ τὴν ὁδὸν αὐτοῦ χαίρων
verses.king_james: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Philippus autem inventus est in Azoto, et pertransiens evangelizabat civitatibus cunctis, donec veniret Cæsaream.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φίλιππος δὲ εὑρέθη εἰς ἄζωτον καὶ διερχόμενος εὐηγγελίζετο τὰς πόλεις πάσας ἕως τοῦ ἐλθεῖν αὐτὸν εἰς καισάρειαν
verses.king_james: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Caesarea.
